Abbott shares rise after securing first win in premature infant formula trial A jury in St. Louis, Missouri found on Thursday that Abbott and Reckitt unit Mead Johnson are not responsible for a young boys severe intestinal disease, marking the first victory for the companies after large losses in two previous trials.  The lawsuit was one of about 1,000 similar cases pending nationwide over allegations the companies failed to warn that their specialized formulas used by newborn intensive care units in hospitals could cause necrotizing enterocolitis (NEC).  NEC almost exclusively affects premature infants and has an estimated mortality rate of more than 20%.
